name: The Crew
post_apply_message:
The Crew (TCU offline) — this recipe does NOT touch the game files (Tony's TCU patch is already applied and working; we keep it as-is). It only:
1. AUTO-ADDS the Steam shortcut ('the crew/thecrew.exe', game folder as start dir, gospel appid 3585568980, the dinput8 override that loads TCU's offline server, GE-Proton 10.34). Steam closes briefly to write it.
2. Installs the LOCAL-SERVER WORKAROUND as a boot service (tcu-network.service): unprivileged port 443 + iptables DNAT of public-ubiservices.ubi.com to 127.0.0.1 (DAKResearcher's script, automated).
Stage GE-Proton 10.34 first with 'gfm stage-runner' so the runner is present (install_runner is optional, so if it's missing everything else still applies and you set Proton manually). If the systemd step fails with a sudo error, run 'passwd' once and re-apply. Verify anytime with 'gfm test-crew'. The offline save (data.bin) lives in the game folder — TODO: add it to the save-capture feature.
notes: The Crew servers died 2024; TCU (thecrewunlimited.com) revives it via a local offline server inside dinput8.dll (already installed in Tony's game dir — 'the crew/' subfolder). REWORKED 2026-07-10 per Tony: DO NOT mod the game (dropped the TCUServer fetch + the patch/config/tculauncher copy_files) — it's working and we keep it. Recipe now = (1) install_runner GE-Proton10-34, (2) tcu-network.service = DAKResearcher's script automated (sysctl unprivileged port 443 + iptables DNAT public-ubiservices.ubi.com -> 127.0.0.1), (3) steam_shortcut auto-add. Game is NESTED at <folder>/the crew/thecrew.exe (lowercase subfolder), so exe/start_dir/marker all point there. Gospel appid = 3585568980 (Tony's new working non-Steam shortcut; old 3251365350 prefix went bad). Save = data.bin in the game folder (not the prefix) — needs the save-capture feature. Health check: gfm test-crew.
